experiment: freeze-restore throughput audit + CRC/cascade bottleneck isolation
notes: All outputs verified coherent / bit-consistent. Snapshots written to the 500GB container overlay (the /workspace network volume has a 20GB quota). gpu_memory_utilization 0.90-0.92, max_model_len 2048-4096, enforce_eager.

qwen7b_tp1_pread_verifyON.note: default one-shot path; CRC on critical path

qwen32b_tp1_mmap_verifyOFF.note: restore_model_tp cascade tries mmap first; cold = page-fault bound

qwen72b_tp2_pread_verifyOFF.note: 145GB model restored in ~5s; per-GPU rate matches 32B, TP=2 doubles aggregate, no NVMe contention on this pod

dma_h2d_gb_s_prestaged_ram.note: PCIe Gen5 H2D from pinned-registered RAM, near line rate

findings: CRC verification (THAW_VERIFY) sits on the freeze/restore critical path single-threaded, capping throughput at ~0.9 GB/s. Disabling it gives 14.3x faster restore (0.88->12.56) and 7.9x faster freeze (0.55->4.34), still bit-consistent. The TP>1 path already sets THAW_VERIFY=0; the TP=1 / one-shot path does not, so default single-GPU users silently eat the tax.

restore_model_tp's cascade tries the mmap (restore_model_from_ram) path first, which is correct for TP>1 NVMe contention but ~7x slower than O_DIRECT pread for cold single-GPU loads (2.09 vs 14.62 GB/s at 32B). TP=1 should prefer pread.

On the fast path (pread + verify off), per-GPU restore is a steady ~12-15 GB/s from 7B through 72B; it does not degrade with model size. TP=2 doubles aggregate to 28.8 GB/s. A 72B (145GB) model restores in ~5s.
The 'Nx vs normal load' multiplier is baseline-dependent (cold-download normal load 133s vs warm 12.7s for 7B); GB/s is the reproducible metric, the multiplier is not.
implied_fixes: Move CRC off the restore/freeze critical path (verify async or truly parallel), or default THAW_VERIFY=0 for one-shot loads as TP>1 already does.
Make the TP=1 restore cascade prefer the O_DIRECT pread path over mmap-first.
